prompt: Japanese Edo-period ukiyo-e woodblock print style, in the manner of Katsushika Hokusai's landscape series. A dramatic mountain landscape with multiple jagged peaks of varying heights rising across the composition. Deep valleys cut between the peaks, with one lowest valley at the center-bottom of the composition — a small solitary figure in traditional clothing descends a winding path toward it. Several shallower side-valleys are visible as distractions along the route. The mountain surfaces use flat unmodulated areas of indigo blue, ochre yellow, and white — no photographic shading, no gradients. Bold black ink outlines define every form. Flattened perspective, layered depth, no Western 3D rendering. Strong diagonal compositional force from upper-left to lower-right. Muted sky in pale cream. Visible woodgrain texture in flat color areas. No text, no labels, no annotations. Single frame.
tradition_approach: Hokusai-style landscape composition with flat unmodulated color blocks, bold black ink outlines, flattened non-Western perspective, limited indigo-ochre-vermillion palette, and diagonal compositional force.
